title: Daily Briefing — Apr 29
headline: Flows bullish, but cracks showing in rotation and breadth — patience is king.
body: Right, so the big picture: Markets are still riding the bull wave with SPY at $711.69, well above its 20 MA ($689.02) and VIX hanging around normal at 17.97, but we're no longer in extreme greed (Fear & Greed down to 63.8 from last week's 68.6, coming off an extreme low last month). Tech is leading—AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, GOOGL, AMZN all sitting miles above key moving averages. Momentum strong, but weekly rotation hints at sector fatigue.

Key sector action: Energy (XLE $57.71) and Materials (XLB $51.40) have rotated to 'Weakening' and 'Leading' respectively on RRG, but watch Industrials (XLI $170.98) and Financials (XLF $51.85)—they're in elevated risk and improving, but not breaking out, which is classic late-cycle behaviour. Real Estate (XLRE $43.91) is triggered and leading, Utilities (XLU $46.25) starting to fade—classic defensive creep.

Contrarian/risk angle: We're not in dumb-money mania yet, but several stocks (ARM down -8%, DELL -4.7%, AA -6.2%) are flashing real volatility even as SPY and QQQ march higher. Retail is cooling off (Fear & Greed back from 14.5 lows), but risk pockets are building in high-beta names. If you missed the tech run, don't chase—abundance mindset. Watch for cracks under the surface; breadth is starting to lag, so cycles within cycles here.
callout_body:
SPY: $711.69 (20 MA $689.02)
QQQ: $657.55 (20 MA $625.35)
XLF: $51.85 (watch 20 MA $51.27)
XLK: $157.85 (20 MA $147.82)
ARM: $198.65 — high volatility, 20 MA $170.20, caution
HYG: $80.40 (20 MA $80.17) — if HYG/LQD break, reduce equity risk
action_items: Stay long core tech (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, XLK) as long as daily closes remain above 20 MA—don't chase after missed moves.
Rotate partial focus to defensive (XLRE, XLU, XLV) and monitor Financials (XLF $51.85). If any break below 20 MA, reduce exposure—especially if HYG/LQD roll over.
closing_quote: If it's in the press, it's already in the price. Patience, react, don't predict.
